quote_of_day.text: China. Shows body discovery of 25 year old young woman who died as the result of erotic asphyxiation. Worked as a medical nurse and online model. Lubricated vibrator was found lying at her feet. Her va-ginal and an-al passages had the same lubricant in them.
quote_of_day.name: Anon

guest_letters.title: Send your articles to the East of Indus newspaper
guest_letters.body: By adding the prefix !eastofindus to your post, and writing your title after that, you can send an article through induschan, which will be published on iac and displayed in the next edition of the newspaper, in the morning or the evening! There is no censorship, there is quality control, and there is a 50 word minimum, send your writing!!!
guest_letters.name: India Abstract Culture

articles.title: Weather for Indus today
articles.body:
A toxic high pressure system of caste hatred dominated the board, spawning violent threats against low castes. This noxious front brought squalls of vicious replies, with Anon openly calling for brutal actions including murder, acid attacks, and other forms of violence.

A scattered drizzle of erotic asphyxiation deaths reports emerged, with one anon sharing a case from Italy involving a 24-year-old Onlyfans and PornHub model who died during a livestream. This somber development hung over the discussion like a fog.

Meanwhile, a debate on incel to Chad transformation blew in, with one participant seeking advice on achieving such a transformation in two months.

A ponderous discussion on the genetic component to sexual preferences also continued, with an anon questioning whether a genetic factor could explain differing sexual inclinations among men.

articles.title: Anon Calls for Violence Against Low Castes, Others Join In
articles.body:
Anon opened the thread with a post calling for violence against low castes, including killing, beheading, and using them as manure.

The poster listed several brutal actions, including dropping low castes in a vat of acid, throwing them into a meat grinder, and gassing them.

Another anon responded by claiming that low castes are responsible for India's poverty and that they have ruined Indian institutions and colleges. This poster also described low castes as "dirty, smelly" and implied they are to blame for India's poor reputation.

A third poster brought up the topic of low castes using upper caste (UC) surnames and linked it to cases of rape.

This poster argued that allowing low castes to use UC surnames leads to confusion and that low castes are responsible for their own actions. One respondent suggested that if low castes were not allowed to use UC surnames, society would be more stable.

This poster also mentioned that upper castes and low castes are not significantly different in their actions. The discussion took a darker turn with some posters making claims about the genetic makeup of low castes and their propensity for criminal behavior.

One poster mentioned a case where a dalit man was accused of raping a 5-year-old and claimed that the entire "chuddist and bhim team" abandoned him.

Some posters expressed support for the idea of reserving certain rights or benefits for certain castes, while others made derogatory comments about low castes and Muslims.

One poster suggested that OBCs (Other Backward Classes) and low castes will eventually surpass upper castes in terms of wealth due to reservation policies. The thread also included some personal anecdotes and insults directed at individuals based on their caste or social status.

The tone of the discussion was largely hostile and divisive, with many posters expressing extreme views on caste and social hierarchy.

articles.title: Anon shares cases of erotic asphyxiation deaths
articles.body:
Anon opened the thread with a story about a 24-year-old Onlyfans and PornHub erotic gay model who died during a livestream in Italy during the COVID-19 pandemic.

The model was using a high-quality pirated VPN, which delayed the discovery of his location and corpse by at least 24 hours.

Another anon shared a case from a 2004 German Forensic Medicine textbook about a 24-year-old man who intentionally caused the death of his 28-year-old gay partner during a strangulation fetish session.

The 24-year-old was acting as a safe spotter but encouraged the partner to push the session further. A respondent described a case of a 23-year-old woman who died during an erotic asphyxiation session with her male partner.

He had waited too long to intervene, leaving her hanging for a minute while he masturbated. Anon also shared the story of an 18-year-old man who streamed his own death on an erotic platform.

A viewer had requested that he masturbate while hanging himself, and the man used a bed sheet to create a makeshift noose.

Some anons discussed the psychology behind erotic asphyxiation, with one suggesting that people get aroused by imagining their own death by hanging or strangulation. Another anon noted that the brain deprived of blood can create a hallucinogenic effect, leading to powerful orgasms.

The thread also included personal reactions, with one respondent stating that they had been watching gore since they were 13 but found hanging videos to be particularly uncomfortable.

Another anon shared that they had almost died during an erotic asphyxiation session but were saved when the noose broke. The discussion also touched on the discovery of fetishes, with one anon wondering how people acquire a taste for erotic asphyxiation.

A respondent suggested that it often starts with past traumas or behavior.

articles.title: Incel to Chad Transformation: A Debate
articles.body:
Anon kicked off the discussion with a bold claim: an incel-to-Chad transformation in just two months.

The OP asked, "HOW?" One respondent suggested that getting in shape was achievable within that timeframe, but changing one's personality was a much harder task, and potentially impossible for those with autism. Another anon offered a straightforward solution: "GET FUCKED, INCEL.

JUST DO IT. GO TO THE GYM. EAT PROTEIN. SLEEP. GROW A BEARD. GET A HAIRCUT. STOP FAP. STOP BITCHING. STOP WHINING.

STOP BEING A FUCKING LOSER." The conversation took a different turn when an anon asked about the difference between thongs and G-strings, with one respondent explaining that thongs covered a little part of the ass, while G-strings were for those who wanted to show their ass.

Some anons shared their personal experiences and advice. One anon mentioned that they had lost their virginity at 25 and were now in love, but struggled with financial stability.

They wished to provide a better life for their partner, but acknowledged the challenges of their current situation. The discussion also touched on the topic of vocabulary and language usage.

One anon advised against using cheap Hindi abuses, especially in front of women, and suggested identifying promiscuous women without openly labeling them.

The thread also included some humorous exchanges, with one anon joking about being a "confirmed fagyush" and another responding with "kek faggot, you are wrong." The conversation concluded with various anons sharing their thoughts on self-improvement, relationships, and personal growth, with no clear consensus on the possibility of an incel-to-Chad transformation in just two months.

articles.title: Anon Ponders Genetic Component to Sexual Preferences
articles.body:
Anon questioned whether a genetic component influences certain sexual preferences or behaviors. They wondered if this explains why some men, particularly those from specific castes, do not develop certain inclinations despite similar exposure.

Anon discussed the idea of a genetic component to sexual preferences, mentioning the possibility that it could explain differences in behavior. Another poster brought up the topic of interracial relationships and sexual performance.

Anon also distinguished between being a "stag" and a "cuck," with the former involving a dominant role in a relationship and the latter involving humiliation.

One user listed specific criteria for a partner, including physical attributes and racial background.

articles.title: Anon posts 'Gobarida edition'
articles.body:
Anon started a thread with the phrase 'Gobarida edition'. Anon and other respondents were unclear what 'Gobarida edition' referred to.

Some thought it might relate to biogas or renewable energy, while others believed it could be a term from a specific cultural or literary context. The conversation quickly diverged into unrelated topics.

One respondent fantasized about a 'bio-mechanical hybrid flesh robot waifu'. Others discussed Indian social dynamics, with one poster expressing desire for 'down to the soil, salt of the earth kind of girls'.

The thread included various inquires and tangents, but no clear explanation of 'Gobarida edition' emerged.
